panCategories.panCategory: Used for natural persons, including resident individuals, non-resident individuals, OCI holders, and sole proprietors where PAN is issued in the proprietor's individual capacity.
Used for companies, including domestic companies, foreign companies, regulated corporate bodies, and many institutional investors structured as companies.
Used for partnership firms and limited liability partnerships.
Used for associations of persons, societies, cooperative bodies, clubs, and similar collective bodies.
Used for trusts including private, public, charitable, mutual fund, pension, and investment trust structures.
Used for government departments and government bodies.
Used for local authorities such as municipal corporations and panchayats.
Used for artificial juridical persons and statutory bodies not otherwise covered.

entityType.name: Individual
entityType.description: An individual is a natural person. Tax residence, citizenship status, and sole proprietorship status do not create a separate PAN category; the PAN category remains P.

subtype.name: Resident and Ordinarily Resident (ROR)
subtype.description: Individual tax residential status for a person who is resident and ordinarily resident in India.

subtype.name: Resident but Not Ordinarily Resident (RNOR)
subtype.description: Individual tax residential status for a person who is resident but not ordinarily resident in India.

subtype.name: Non-Resident Individual (NR)
subtype.description: A natural person who is non-resident for Indian tax purposes.

subtype.name: Sole Proprietorship
subtype.registration: GSTIN / Udyam / Shop and Establishment Registration
subtype.description: A business carried on by an individual proprietor. PAN is generally the individual's PAN and therefore category P.

subtype.name: Overseas Citizen of India (OCI)
subtype.description: OCI is a citizenship or immigration status of a natural person and not a separate legal entity category.

entityType.name: Hindu Undivided Family
entityType.description: A Hindu Undivided Family is a separate taxable unit recognized under Indian tax law and identified by PAN category H.

subtype.name: Hindu Undivided Family (HUF)
subtype.description: Family unit consisting of lineal descendants and managed by the karta.

entityType.name: Company
entityType.aliases: company,corporate,body corporate,limited company,private limited,public limited,corporation,CIN,foreign company
entityType.description: A company is a corporate legal person. In PAN classification, companies are generally mapped to fourth character C, including many regulated corporate investors.

subtype.name: Domestic Ordinary Company
subtype.description: An Indian company incorporated under the Companies Act.

subtype.name: Domestic One Person Company (OPC)
subtype.description: A one person company incorporated under Indian company law.

subtype.name: Producer Company
subtype.description: A company formed by producers, farmers, or producer institutions under the Companies Act framework.

subtype.name: Nidhi Company
subtype.description: A Nidhi company is incorporated to cultivate thrift and savings among its members.

subtype.name: Section 8 Company
subtype.description: A not-for-profit company licensed under Section 8 of the Companies Act.

subtype.name: Foreign Company / Overseas Corporate Body (OCB)
subtype.registration: RBI FCRN / MCA FCRN / RBI Approval
subtype.description: A foreign company or overseas corporate body recognized for Indian regulatory or investment purposes.

entityType.name: Firm / LLP
entityType.description: Partnership firms and LLPs are generally mapped to PAN fourth character F. The registration differs depending on whether the structure is an ordinary partnership or LLP.

subtype.name: Unlimited Liability Firm / Partnership Firm
subtype.registration: Partnership Deed / Registrar of Firms Registration Number
subtype.description: An ordinary partnership firm formed by partners under a partnership deed.

subtype.name: Limited Liability Partnership (LLP)
subtype.description: A limited liability partnership registered under the LLP Act.

subtype.name: Joint Venture structured as Firm / LLP
subtype.description: A joint venture is an arrangement and may be legally structured as a partnership firm or LLP.

entityType.name: Trust
entityType.aliases: trust,public trust,private trust,charitable trust,mutual fund,AIF,NPS,pension trust,endowment
entityType.description: A trust is a fiduciary structure and is generally mapped to PAN category T. Many pooled investment structures such as mutual funds, AIFs, and pension funds may use trust form.

subtype.name: Trust
subtype.description: A private, public, religious, or charitable trust formed under a trust deed or relevant public trust law.

subtype.name: Mutual Fund (MF)
subtype.description: Indian mutual funds are commonly organized as trusts and regulated by SEBI.

subtype.name: Alternative Investment Fund as Trust
subtype.description: An AIF may be constituted as a trust, company, or LLP. This subtype represents the trust form.

subtype.name: Local Pension Fund - NPS Trust
subtype.description: NPS-related trust or pension structure regulated by PFRDA.

subtype.name: Charitable Organisation as Trust
subtype.registration: Trust Deed / Charity Commissioner / Relevant Registration
subtype.description: A charitable organization legally constituted as a trust.

entityType.name: AOP / BOI / Society
entityType.legalStatus: Association of Persons / Body of Individuals
entityType.description: Associations, societies, cooperative bodies, and bodies of individuals are generally mapped to PAN categories A or B depending on legal constitution.

subtype.name: Association of Persons (AOP)
subtype.description: A group of persons joining for a common purpose, often assessed as an AOP.

subtype.name: Body of Individuals (BOI)
subtype.description: A body consisting of individuals, generally mapped to PAN category B.

subtype.name: Society
subtype.description: A society registered under applicable societies registration law.

subtype.name: Cooperative Society
subtype.description: A cooperative society registered under state cooperative law.

subtype.name: Multi-State Cooperative Society
subtype.description: A cooperative society operating in more than one state and registered under the Multi-State Cooperative Societies law.

subtype.name: Corporate Housing Society
subtype.description: A housing society commonly formed as a cooperative body.

subtype.name: Residents Welfare Association (RWA)
subtype.description: An association representing residents of a locality, complex, or housing community.

entityType.name: Government / Juridical Person
entityType.description: Government bodies are generally mapped to PAN category G. Certain statutory or artificial juridical persons may be mapped to J.

subtype.name: Government - Central
subtype.description: A department, ministry, or body of the Central Government.

subtype.name: Government - State
subtype.description: A department, ministry, or body of a State Government.

subtype.name: Government - Others
subtype.description: Other government, statutory, juridical, or local public bodies depending on constitution.

subtype.name: Local Pension Fund - EPFO
subtype.registration: Statutory Act Registration / EPFO Establishment Details
subtype.description: EPFO or similar statutory pension institution categorized as government or juridical depending on the specific holder.

entityType.name: Local Authority
entityType.description: Local authorities such as municipal corporations and panchayats are generally mapped to PAN category L.

subtype.name: Government - Local Authority - Municipal Corporation
subtype.description: A municipal corporation or municipality established under applicable law.

subtype.name: Government - Local Authority - Panchayats
subtype.description: A panchayat or rural local authority established under applicable law.

entityType.name: Regulated Financial Entity
entityType.legalStatus: Usually Company / Trust / LLP depending on constitution
entityType.description: Regulated financial institutions are categorized according to their legal constitution, usually as companies, trusts, or LLPs.

subtype.name: Bank
subtype.description: A banking company or banking institution regulated by RBI.

subtype.name: Non-Banking Financial Company (NBFC)
subtype.description: A non-banking financial company registered with RBI.

subtype.name: Insurance Company
subtype.description: An insurance or reinsurance company regulated by IRDAI.

subtype.name: Asset Reconstruction Company
subtype.description: An asset reconstruction company registered with RBI.

subtype.name: Portfolio Manager
subtype.description: A portfolio manager regulated by SEBI and structured as a company or LLP.

subtype.name: Alternative Investment Fund as Company / LLP
subtype.description: An AIF constituted as a company or LLP instead of a trust.

group.name: Foreign Investors, FPIs, FVCIs and Overseas Offices

entityType.name: Foreign Portfolio Investor (FPI)
entityType.legalStatus: Foreign Investor - Legal status depends on constitution
entityType.aliases: FPI,foreign portfolio investor,foreign fund,offshore fund,sovereign wealth fund,pension fund,endowment,foundation,SEBI FPI
entityType.description: FPI is a regulatory investor category under SEBI. The PAN category depends on the legal nature of the FPI, such as company, trust, AOP, firm, government, juridical person, or individual.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Central Bank
subtype.registration: SEBI FPI Registration Number plus home regulator details
subtype.description: A central bank or similar monetary authority registered as Category I FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Sovereign Wealth Fund
subtype.description: A sovereign wealth fund eligible as Category I FPI.
subtype.keywords: sovereign wealth fund,SWF,FPI category I,government fund

subtype.name: FPI Category I - International / Multilateral Organization / Agency
subtype.description: An international or multilateral agency registered as Category I FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Entity at least 75% Government owned or controlled
subtype.description: An entity controlled or at least 75% owned by government and eligible as Category I FPI.
subtype.keywords: government owned,75 percent,controlled entity,FPI category I

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Pension Fund
subtype.description: A pension fund eligible for Category I FPI registration.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - University Fund
subtype.description: A university fund eligible for Category I FPI registration.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Appropriately Regulated Insurance / Reinsurance Entity
subtype.description: An appropriately regulated insurance or reinsurance entity registered as FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Appropriately Regulated Bank
subtype.description: An appropriately regulated bank registered as Category I FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Appropriately Regulated Asset Management Company
subtype.description: An appropriately regulated asset management company eligible as Category I FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Appropriately Regulated Investment Manager
subtype.description: An appropriately regulated investment manager structured as company or firm.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Appropriately Regulated Investment Advisor
subtype.description: An appropriately regulated investment adviser or advisor.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Appropriately Regulated Portfolio Manager
subtype.description: An appropriately regulated portfolio manager registered as FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Appropriately Regulated Broker Dealer
subtype.description: An appropriately regulated broker dealer registered as FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Appropriately Regulated Swap Dealer
subtype.description: An appropriately regulated swap dealer registered as FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Appropriately Regulated Fund
subtype.description: An appropriately regulated fund eligible for Category I FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Unregulated Fund whose Investment Manager is Category I FPI
subtype.description: An unregulated fund eligible due to its investment manager being Category I FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - University Related Endowment
subtype.description: A university-related endowment eligible as Category I FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Entity whose Investment Manager is Category I FPI from FATF jurisdiction
subtype.description: An entity eligible due to its investment manager being an eligible Category I FPI from a FATF member jurisdiction.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Entity at least 75% owned by Pension Fund
subtype.description: An entity substantially owned by an eligible pension fund.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Entity at least 75% owned by University Fund
subtype.description: An entity substantially owned by an eligible university fund.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Entity at least 75% owned by Appropriately Regulated Entity
subtype.description: An entity substantially owned by an appropriately regulated entity.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Entity at least 75% owned by University Related Endowment
subtype.description: An entity substantially owned by a university-related endowment.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Appropriately Regulated Stock Exchange
subtype.description: An appropriately regulated stock exchange registered as FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Appropriately Regulated Clearing Corporation
subtype.description: An appropriately regulated clearing corporation registered as FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category I - Appropriately Regulated Central Securities Depository
subtype.description: An appropriately regulated central securities depository registered as FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category II - Appropriately Regulated Fund not eligible as Category I FPI
subtype.description: An appropriately regulated fund registered as Category II FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category II - Endowment / Foundation
subtype.description: An endowment or foundation registered as Category II FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category II - Charitable Organisation
subtype.description: A charitable organization registered as Category II FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category II - Corporate Body
subtype.description: A foreign corporate body registered as Category II FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category II - Family Office
subtype.description: A family office registered as Category II FPI depending on its constitution.

subtype.name: FPI Category II - Individual FPI
subtype.registration: SEBI FPI Registration Number plus Passport
subtype.description: An individual foreign investor registered as Category II FPI.

subtype.name: FPI Category II - Regulated Entity investing on behalf of Client
subtype.description: An appropriately regulated entity investing on behalf of clients.

subtype.name: FPI Category II - Unregulated Fund - Limited Partnership / Trust
subtype.description: An unregulated fund structured as a limited partnership or trust.

subtype.name: FPI Category II - Others
subtype.description: Other eligible Category II FPI applicants depending on their legal constitution.

entityType.name: Foreign Venture Capital Investor / Foreign Office
entityType.aliases: FVCI,foreign venture capital investor,branch office,liaison office,liason office,project office,foreign office,RBI approval
entityType.description: Foreign venture capital investors and Indian offices of foreign entities are classified according to their legal constitution. Branch, liaison, and project offices generally relate to a foreign company.

subtype.name: Foreign Venture Capital Investor (FVCI)
subtype.description: A foreign venture capital investor registered with SEBI.

subtype.name: Branch Office
subtype.description: An Indian branch office of a foreign company. It is an office type, not a separate legal entity category.

subtype.name: Liaison Office
subtype.description: An Indian liaison office of a foreign company. Also commonly misspelled as liason office.

subtype.name: Project Office
subtype.description: An Indian project office of a foreign company set up for a specific project.

entityType.name: Investment Route / Instrument
entityType.legalStatus: Route or Instrument, not standalone legal entity
entityType.description: FDI, FCCB, and Depository Receipts are investment routes or instruments. PAN is normally based on the underlying investor, issuer, or company rather than the instrument itself.

subtype.name: Foreign Direct Investment (FDI)
subtype.description: FDI is an investment route, usually involving a foreign investor and an Indian investee company.

subtype.name: Depository Receipts
subtype.description: Depository receipts are instruments linked to an underlying issuer company.

subtype.name: Foreign Currency Convertible Bond (FCCB)
subtype.description: FCCB is a debt or convertible instrument issued by a company, not a separate legal entity.

subtype.name: Joint Venture structured as Company
subtype.description: A joint venture is a business arrangement and may be structured as a company.
